scenario: Helios Bank coordinates 9000 tasks per day in a Camunda 8.8 onboarding flow. The modeler configures a zeebe:taskListener on the assigning event with a static type value and omits the retries attribute, expecting the documented platform default for user task listener jobs to apply for failures handled by the worker.
question: What default number of retries applies to a user task listener job when the retries attribute is omitted on zeebe:taskListener?

options.text: A default of three retries applies to the user task listener job when the retries attribute is omitted on the zeebe:taskListener element in the BPMN model
A default of five retries applies to the user task listener job when the retries attribute is omitted on the zeebe:taskListener element in the BPMN model
A default of zero retries applies to the user task listener job when the retries attribute is omitted on the zeebe:taskListener element in the BPMN model
A default of one retry applies to the user task listener job when the retries attribute is omitted on the zeebe:taskListener element in the BPMN model

a.text: Correct. The user task listeners documentation states the retries attribute is optional and defaults to 3 when omitted on a zeebe:taskListener element, matching the standard job retry default.

b.text: Incorrect. No documented default of 5 retries exists for user task listener jobs; the documented default is 3 retries when the retries attribute is left off.

c.text: Incorrect. Zero retries would mean a single failure raises an incident immediately, which is not the documented default for user task listener jobs.

d.text: Incorrect. A default of 1 retry is not what the documentation specifies; user task listener jobs follow the same 3-retry default as other job types.

explanation: When the optional retries attribute is omitted from a zeebe:taskListener element, the user task listener job is created with the documented default of 3 retries.
